title: 151 Mathematics 2.   Each part of Fig. 2, has been obtained on dividing   1
subject: Mathematics
unit: Unit L - cardboard as shown in Fig
activityTitle: 151 Mathematics 2.   Each part of Fig. 2, has been obtained on dividing   1
objective: To multiply integers   using unit squares of different colours
materials:
Cardboard, white paper, red and blue grid papers,
colour pens (Red and Blue), adhesive, ruler, scissors.
procedure:
1.   Take a cardboard of a convenient size and paste a white paper on it.
2.   Take a blue grid paper and cut out sufficient number of
square pieces of unit squares. Each blue square represents
an integer ‘+1’ (Fig. 1).
3.   Take a red grid paper   and cut out sufficient number of
square pieces of unit area. Let each red square represent
an integer ‘–1’ (Fig. 2).
4.   Paste one blue unit square and one red unit square together so that
one side of the square is blue and the other is red.
observation:
I.   For two positive integers, say 2   ×   3.
1.   Draw 5 ( = 2 + 3) blue edges of   unit length on this
cardboard as shown in Fig. 3.
2.   Complete the rectangular shape using blue unit
squares as shown in Fig. 4.
discussion:
This activity is useful in explaining division of a fraction by a natural number.
152 Laboratory Manual – Elementary Stage
